name: Holiday Fruitcake Cookies
recipeInstructions:
1. Sift together flour, baking soda, salt, and cinnamon. Set aside.
2. Beat shortening until light; add sugar and egg, beating until fluffy.
3. Add buttermilk alternately with dry ingredients to the shortening mixture; mix well.
4. Fold in dates, nuts, and cherries. Cover bowl with plastic wrap and refrigerate dough until chilled, about 2 to 3 hours.
5. Preheat oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
6. Drop dough by teaspoonfuls, about 2 inches apart, onto a lightly greased cookie sheet.
7. Bake about 8 to 10 minutes or until lightly browned. Let cool on wire racks.
Note: You can substitute 8 ounces pitted dates, cut into small pieces.
Yield: About 5 dozen cookies.
